model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Illinois holds a commanding 150+ spot advantage in advanced metrics with elite national rankings of 6th on Torvik and 7th on KenPom, driven by the nation's top-ranked offense and a top-25 defense. The Illini's +32.14 efficiency margin dwarfs Penn's +0.8 mark, representing a gap of over 30 efficiency points that suggests a mismatch of historic proportions. Penn enters with a modest 17-11 record from the Ivy League and ranks in the 150s nationally, facing a Big Ten-tested Illinois squad that has navigated the nation's toughest conference while maintaining elite two-way efficiency. The 3/14 seed line historically produces victories for the favorite approximately 85% of the time, and Illinois' profile as one of the strongest 3-seeds provides minimal vulnerability for Penn to exploit.

reasoningStep.title: Statistical Profile Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Illinois demonstrates elite national-caliber metrics with top-10 rankings and the #1 offense in the country, while Penn profiles as a below-average tournament team with middling efficiency numbers and weak schedule competition from the Ivy League. The 30+ point efficiency margin gap and 150+ ranking spot differential represent a mismatch that aligns with historical 3/14 outcomes strongly favoring the favorite.
evidence: Illinois: KenPom #7, Torvik #6, Adj EM +32.14, Offense #1 nationally, Defense #24
Penn: KenPom #159, Torvik #153, Adj EM +0.8, Offense #215, Defense #103
Illinois record 24-8 (Big Ten), Penn record 17-11 (Ivy League)

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

modelTrace.content:
The web searches are failing, but I have excellent data from the ratings lookup. Let me analyze what I have:

**Illinois:**
- KenPom Rank: 7th nationally
- Torvik Rank: 6th nationally
- Record: 24-8
- Conference: Big Ten (B10)
- Adjusted Efficiency Margin: +32.14 (elite)
- Offense: Ranked 1st nationally in adjusted offense (131.2)
- Defense: Ranked 24th nationally (99.1)

**Penn:**
- KenPom Rank: 159th nationally
- Torvik Rank: 153rd nationally
- Record: 17-11
- Conference: Ivy League
- Adjusted Efficiency Margin: +0.8 (barely above average)
- Offense: Ranked 215th nationally
- Defense: Ranked 103rd

This is a massive mismatch. Illinois has approximately a 150+ ranking spot advantage. The efficiency margin gap is enormous (+32.14 vs +0.8). Illinois has the #1 offense in the country according to KenPom.

Historical 3/14 matchups favor the 3-seed about 85% of the time. This profile suggests Illinois is one of the strongest 3-seeds possible (top-7 national ranking) while Penn is a below-average tournament team ranking in the 150s.

Without any injury concerns reported (and the web search failing), I should assume both teams are at full strength. The data overwhelmingly favors Illinois.

Based on the advanced metrics data I have gathered, I can now make a well-informed prediction for this matchup.
